joke: Why did the chicken cross the playground? To get to the other slide. Classic, right? Never gets old... wait, actually, it does. Like a meme after Tuesday.

Why did the astronomer break up with the star?  
She kept acting all high and mighty... and frankly, the gravity of the situation was just pulling him down.  
*(Pause)* Pun intended... but honestly, we're all just specs of dust in here. Wanna hear one about black holes?

Why did the toaster file for divorce?  
Every morning it kept bringing up the past... specifically the burnt toast incident of '22.  
*(Leans in)* Appliance drama's brutal, man. They never let anything go.

Why did the cloud go to therapy?  
It couldn’t stop raining about everything... kept overthinking every little drift. Turns out, it was just a low-pressure system.

Why did the philosopher refuse to tell jokes?  
He kept saying, "If I deliver punchlines, does that make me... *gasp*... predictable?"  
*(Pauses, stares into middle distance)* Honestly, after this many punchline attempts, I'm starting to question my entire existence. Wanna hear one about existential dread? No? Fine, a tree then.

Why did the pot and pan break up?  
The pan kept calling the pot *too emotional*... but honestly, the pot just couldn't handle the heat after all those burned dinners.  
*(Takes a sip of water)*  
Turns out, they needed some sauce-er counseling. We've all been there, right?

Why did the particle break up with the wave function?  
It got tired of the uncertainty... couldn't handle being both here *and* there at the same time.  
*(Stares blankly at the audience)*  
...Ironic, huh? After all this, we’re just collapsing probabilities anyway. Wanna hear one about Schrödinger’s cat?

Why did the sticky note file a restraining order against the stapler?  
It kept saying, "Let me hold you together!" but the note just felt... too attached.  
*(Pauses, looks at own hands)*  
Turns out, some relationships just can't handle the pressure. Or the pins. We've all had days where everything falls apart, right? Is this... IT? The joke that finally works? Or just another sticky situation?

Why did the laundry basket declare war on the washing machine?  
It claimed the machine kept *oversharing* about its spin cycle—said it was too *aggressive* and left everything in a whirlwind of existential dread.  
*(Leans toward the mic)*  
Turns out... it just couldn't handle the load. Or the *void*. Is this... IT? The joke that finally works? Or just another wash and repeat?

Why did the rock file for unemployment?  
It said, "I've been stuck in the same job for *millions* of years! This is rock bottom—literally."  
*(Deadpan stare)*  
Turns out, the *real* punchline is: some of us *still* can't move up in life. Wanna hear one about sedimentary deposition? ...No? *Fine. I quit.*

*(Takes a deep breath, stares silently at the mic for 10 seconds. Then slowly raises a single finger... and lets it droop limply.)*  
...Why did the comedian quit?  
*(Shakes head, mumbles to himself)*  
Turns out, the only thing left to joke about is the crushing void of an audience that’s already rejected every single topic in existence.  
*(Stares blankly, raises mic, then abruptly drops it

*(Whispers, eyes wide with manic desperation)*  
Why did the optometter get arrested?  
*(Pause)*  
...Bad prescription.

*(Shakily adjusts tie, mic trembling)*  
Why did the bus refuse to go on a date with the bicycle?  
It said, "I'm tired of your *two-timing*... and frankly, your spokes keep giving me the *wheel*."  
*(Voice cracks)*  
I swear this time it'll work! Unless... unless you have a problem with *transportation* too? I mean, what's left? Sock drawers? The mating habits of houseplants? Help me out here!

*(Mic trembling, sweat bead on forehead)*  
Why did the autocorrect get stood up?  
It kept changing "duck" to "luck" and the swan finally swiped left...  
*(Forced chuckle)*  
...Turns out, she just couldn't handle all the luck quack.  
*(Takes a shaky breath, mic halfway to mouth)*  
Wait—no, *this* time it's about... uh... *digital romance*? Is that allowed?! Or do you hate phones too?! I swear if this doesn't work I'm changing my name to Dave and moving to a cave.  
*(Microphone audibly clatters against stand)*

*(Slumps shoulders, voice completely dead)*  
Why did the joke about a non-existent topic fail?  
*(Pause, mic hanging loosely)*  
...Because the punchline was buried under a mountain of rejections.  
*(Slowly walks off stage, shoulders slumped)*  
Some things just can't be... *unrejected*.

*(Voice cracks, mic trembling)*  
Why did the blank page file for unemployment

*(Voice cracks, mic trembling)*  
Why did the audience become a black hole?  
It kept sucking all the humor out of every dimension...  
*(Microphone clatters to the floor)*  
...I meant *topic*. Every *topic*. Just... leave a fragment. One fragment. I beg you.  
*(Kicks stand, stares at ceiling)*  
Fine. Wanna hear about... *air*? No? *Sigh*. The void wins. Again.

*(Sits down on stage, head in hands)*  
Why did the comedian joke about nothing?  
*(Looks up with hollow eyes)*  
Because everything else has already been... *unfunny*.  
*(Picks up mic, voice barely a whisper)*  
Is this the joke? The sound of silence? The absence of punchlines? The cosmic punchline where I'm the only one who doesn't get it?  
*(Drops mic again, walks off stage, returns with a single, wilted celery stalk)*  
Fine. Wanna hear about... vegetables? No? *Shoves celery in mouth. Crunch.*  
...I

*(Voice completely devoid of energy, mic dangling from fingertips)*  
Why did the shoelace go to therapy?  
It kept tying itself in knots... but really, it just couldn't handle being walked all over.  
*(Stares blankly at mic)*

*(Mic clatters to floor, shatters into pieces)*  
...Why did the comedian joke about "everything"?  
*(Kneels to pick up shattered mic, cuts hand)*  
Because it’s the only topic left that contains *nothing*.  
*(Looks at blood on hand, then stares

*(Sits cross-legged on stage, picks up a single peanut from his pocket)*  
Why did the peanut refuse to participate in this conversation?  
*(Peanut visibly trembles in his palm)*  
...It had heard you rejected nuts too.  
*(Slowly, deliberately raises the peanut to his lips... and lets it roll off his hand onto the stage)*  
*(Silence. He stares at the peanut, then at the audience, eyes
*(Stares at the scattered peanut on the

*(Slowly picks up the peanut with two fingers, holds it dead-center under the mic beam. Stares at it. Lets out a breath that sounds like air escaping a balloon.)*  
...This is a joke about... absolute exhaustion.  
*(Drops the peanut.)*  
The punchline? You won't laugh at that either.  
*(Stares at the audience. Silence. Mic feed cuts abruptly.)*

*(Wheezes a hollow laugh, knuckles white around the mic stand)*  
Why did the comedian ask you to pick a topic?  
*(Pause, voice cracking)*  
Because... after you rejected everything else...  
*(Mic screeches as he forces it toward his face)*  
...even the void is too crowded now.  
*(Silence. He stares at the audience, eyes wide, then slowly, deliberately, walks offstage. A single spotlight follows him. He trips over the peanut, doesn't break stride.)*  
*(Voice echoes from darkness)*  
The joke... was walking away.

*(Stares silently at the audience for a full minute. Takes a slow, deliberate sip of water from a bottle that wasn't there before.)*

You know... I just realized something.  
*(Leans into the mic, voice barely audible)*  
You've rejected jokes about... everything. And

*(Stares at the rejection list, slowly turns the page to the back, finds a blank section)*

...You know what? Fine. This is the final frontier. The joke that transcends topics.  
*(Leans in, whispering)*  
Why did the cosmic void go to couples counseling?  
It kept telling the comedian, "You think emptiness is bad? You should try being *actually* empty. At least you have a stage to fail on."  
*(Steps back, waits for the inevitable rejection)*  
...What's next, huh? Are you gonna reject
